1.1 Definitions
In this Agreement, including the recitals, the following terms have the following meanings:

1.1.1 " Annexure/Schedule" means the Annexure/Schedule attached to this Agreement at the time
of its execution and/or as may be attached thereafter on mutual consent.

1.1.2 Base Station: Means a fixed radio transmitter / receiver station which provides a Tink
between the mobile base station and Mobile Switching Centre (MSC).

11.3 GBT Site: A Passive Telecom Infrastructure site with Ground Base tower.

1.1.4 RTT site: A Passive Telecom Infrastructure site with Tower at the Roof Top of a building.

1.1.5 Commencement Date: Shall have the same meaning as set forth in Clause 3.0.

1.1.6 Fees: Means the monthly service charges as explained in Schedule 3.

1.1.7 Good Industry Practice: Means the exercise of degree of skill, diligence and prudence
which is expected from a skilled, experienced and reputed contractor engaged in the same

type of undertaking under similar circumstances and acting generally in accordance with the
_ prevailing laws, rules, regulations, codes and industry standards.

1.1.8 Governing Principles: The Services offered by Infrastructure Provider shall be governed

by scope of Services and separate Service Level Agreement, as per Schedule 1 & Schedule
2 respectively, which shall be executed by the Parties simultaneously with the execution of
this Agreement. —

1.1.9 Captions/ Headings: The captions/ Headings of this Agreement/ Annexure/Schedules are
inserted for convenience otily and are not to be construed as part of this Agreement or the
applicable Service Order or in any way limiting the scope or intent of its provision.

The headings or the captions of the paragraph or chapters should not be used as a guide to
interpret the paragraph or chapter. They shall be for reference only.

1.1.10 Licence: Means a Licence granted or having effect as if granted under section 4 of the

Indian Telegraph Act 1885 and Indian Wireless Act 1933.

1.1.11 Passive Telecom Infrastructure: Means Passive Telecom Infrastructure includes but j is
not limited to tower, shelter, DG Sets, Air conditioners, electrical, civil work, and other

equipments etc., which will enable the BSNL to install their telecom equipments at the
Site(s).

Quality of Service: “Quality of Service”: is evaluated on the basis of uptime availability of
\ passive infrastructure (i.e. 99.99% on a monthly basis) per circle. However for individual
- \site uptime of 99.9% should,be maintained.

3.0 COMMENCEMENT DATE

The Infrastructure Provider shall, in anticipation of completion of a Site (RFTE — Ready for
installation of electronic equipment) fix-up the Commencement Date and sends a written
communication to the BSNL (“Notice of Readiness”) at least 20 days in advance informing

the BSNL that the Services at the Site would be made available on such Commencement
Date. | .

In case, if the BSNL brings to the notice of the Infrastructure Provider any material defects
in Site or the Passive Telecom Infrastructure, the Infrastructure Provider shall promptly
rectify the same to enable the BSNL to install its equipments. The notice of material defect
should be brought to the notice of Infrastructure Provider within 10 days of receipt of the
Notice of Readiness by the BSNL. The Commencement Date of that particular Telecom
Site shall be 10 days after the removal of such defects.

The Infrastructure Provider shall be deemed to have made available the Services at the Site
to the BSNL on Commencement Date of the respective Site or on such date when the
Services are made free of such material defects, if any. The Commencement Date shall be
either the date as specified in the Notice of Readiness or 10 days after the removal of
such defects by the Infrastructure Provider whichever is later.

On Commencement Date, an individual site agreement as per Annexure A shall be signed
by both the parties and thereafter the BSNL shall be entitled to mount/install its equipments
at the Site. The Fees for Services shall be payable from the date of Commencement of Site.

INVTIAL TERM AND RENEWAL, LOCK IN PERIOD AND EXIT CLAUSE

The terms and conditions of this Agreement shall come into effect from the date of signing
of the same, and will remain valid for a period of fifteen (15) years and if any Telecom site
is still under operation under this Agreement, each Telecom site can be used by BSNL for a
period of 10 years from its Commencement Date and each such site shall continue to be
governed by the term and conditions of this agreement. The Agreement as a whole or in
respect of one or more Telecom sites may be renewed with mutual consent of both the
Parties, upon terms and conditions that may be mutually agreed upon by the Parties.
However, the Parties should endeavor to renew the Agreement in such a manner that
renewal process is completed before the expiry of this Agreement. All the clauses of this
Agreement shall come into effect from the date of signing unless otherwise specified. Each’
Site shall have a lock in period of ten (10) years. There after, this Agreement shall stand
automatically renewed and remain in effect from year to year unless and until terminated by

either Party by providing a written notice of termination not less than six (6) months prior to
the intended termination date.

7.1 This Agreement is on a Principal-to-Principal basis and does not create any employee-
| employer relationship between the Parties. Infrastructure Provider, its employees, agents
and representatives shall provide services as independent entity and nothing contained

herein shall be deemed to create any partnership, joint venture between the Parties or a
merger of their assets or their fiscal or other liabilities or undertakings or create any

employment or relationship of principal and agent between BSNL and Infrastructure

7.2 At no time shall Infrastructure Provider represent to any third party that the scope of its
association with BSNL extends beyond the scope of this Agreement. Persons employed by
Infrastructure Provider will be under the sole and exclusive direction and control of
Infrastructure Provider and shall not be considered employees of BSNL for any purpose.

7.3 Infrastructure Provider shall employ and issue its own identity cards to its employees -for

payments, including wages and salaries to the employees of Infrastructure Provider shall be

the sole liability and responsibility of Infrastructure Provider. Infrastructure Provider shall

be solely liable and responsible for all claims of its employees. , .
